content.full_text:
State of California
Hair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

May 24, 1976

Paul B. Wells
Procopio, Cory, Hargreaves
and Savitch
1900 California First Bank
Building
530 B Street
San Diego, California 92101

Dear Mr. Wells:

Your letter of May 5, 1976, to John Greenwood has been
referred to me for a response. On the basis of facts stated
in that letter, you have correctly concluded that the San Diego
Construction Industry Advancement Fund (CIAP) will be a "committee"
pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c) if it makes
monetary contributions totalling $5,000 or more in a calendar
year. As long as CIAP engages in no independent campaign activity
and makes no expenditures on non-monetary contributions it is not
a "committee" as defined in Government Code Section 8201.3(b).
Moreover, CIAP is not a committee within the meaning of Government
Code Section 82013(a) because the funds it receives are not
"contributions". You stated that in 1973, CIAP made no reportable
contributions. In 1974, it contributed 4.4% of its income to
committees supporting or opposing ballot measures and in 1975
made no reportable contributions. Based on this prior course of
conduct as well as the arrangement under which individual contractors
make payments to the fund, you have concluded correctly that the
funds received should not be characterized as "contributions".

This informal opinion is based only on the facts stated in
your letter and would not be applicable if CIAP changes its
procedures and begins to engage in political activities on a
regular basis so that the contractor has reason to know that a
portion of his payments will be used to make contributions or
expenditures.

I hope this information will be of assistance.

Sincerely,

Natalie E. West
Staff Counsel

NEW:av
cc: John Greenwood

TECHNICAL ASSISTANCE/ADMINISTRATION (916) 322-5660 • EXECUTIVE/LEGAL 322-5601 • ENFORCEMENT/CONDUCT OF INTEREST 322-5629

The image appears to be a mostly blank page with only a few faint marks or symbols visible at the top. The rest of the page is white/empty. Due to the extremely limited and unclear content, I cannot reliably transcribe specific text.

On the basis of facts stated in that letter, you have correctly concluded that the San Diego Construction Industry Advancement Fund (CIAF) will be a "committee" pursuant to Government Code Section 82013(c) if it makes monetary contributions totalling $5,000 or more in a calendar year. As long as CIAF engages in no independent campaign activity and makes no expenditures or non-monetary contributions it is not a "committee" as defined in Government Code Section 82013(b). Moreover, CIAF is not a committee within the meaning of Government Code Section 82013(a) because the funds it receives are not "contributions". You stated that in 1973, CIAF made no reportable contributions. In 1974, it contributed 4.4% of its income to committees supporting or opposing ballot measures and in 1975 made no reportable contributions. Based on this prior course of conduct as well as the arrangement under which individual contractors make payments to the fund, you have concluded correctly that the funds received should not be characterized as "contributions". This informal opinion is based only on the facts stated in your letter and would not be applicable if CIAF changes its procedures and begins to engage in political activities on a regular basis so that the contractor has reason to know that a portion of his payments will be used to make contributions or expenditures. I hope this information will be of assistance. Sincerely, Natalie E. West Staff Counsel The image you provided is completely blank, with no visible text, numbers, or any other content. It appears to be a white page with two holes at the top, possibly for binding in a report or notebook. If you have another page or section of the document that you would like me to help with, please provide it.
